% An upright portrait page containing a table rotated 90 degrees to fit
% (the common academic-PDF pattern), surrounded by normal upright text.
% No page /Rotate attribute. Used to test sideways-block detection.
\documentclass[12pt]{article}
\usepackage[paperwidth=8.5in,paperheight=11in,margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\pagestyle{empty}
\setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
\begin{document}

This upright paragraph precedes a sideways table. Its characters should
report an angle of zero and cluster into ordinary horizontal lines.

\vspace{1cm}
\begin{center}
\rotatebox{90}{%
\begin{tabular}{|l|r|r|r|}
\hline
Quarter & Revenue & Costs & Margin \\
\hline
Q1 2025 & 1 288 272 & 934 001 & 27.5 \\
Q2 2025 & 1 401 356 & 988 442 & 29.5 \\
Q3 2025 & 1 512 990 & 1 044 213 & 31.0 \\
Q4 2025 & 1 633 105 & 1 101 870 & 32.5 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}}
\end{center}
\vspace{1cm}

This upright paragraph follows the sideways table and should likewise
be detected as normal horizontal text.

\end{document}
